tasks.title: Repository Root Files
tasks.description: Create CLAUDE.md, README.md, TUTORIAL_CHECKLIST.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, .env.example, and root index.html (Docsify loader)
tasks.details:
1. CLAUDE.md: Bot guidance covering project structure, conventions (symbols 🎯⚠️✅💡), Docsify setup, k3d cluster name (goldilocks-demo), registry path, lesson authoring rules.
2. README.md: Tutorial overview with lesson index table, prerequisites (k3d, kubectl, helm), tech stack versions (Goldilocks v4.15.1, Helm chart 10.4.0), estimated time (~3 hours total), getting started instructions.
3. TUTORIAL_CHECKLIST.md: Per-lesson verification checklists with specific kubectl commands to confirm success.
4. CONTRIBUTING.md: How to contribute lessons, validation requirements, PR process.
5. .env.example: CLUSTER_NAME=goldilocks-demo, GOLDILOCKS_NAMESPACE=goldilocks, APP_NAMESPACE=metrics-app.
6. index.html: Root Docsify loader with golden theme (#f5a623), lists all lessons with links.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify all files exist. CLAUDE.md reviewed for accuracy. README.md index links to all 9 lesson folders. index.html renders without JS errors when served with npx serve.

tasks.title: Setup Scripts
tasks.description: Create and validate all scripts in scripts/: setup-cluster.sh, verify-prerequisites.sh, verify-goldilocks.sh, deploy-sample-app.sh, cleanup.sh
tasks.details:
All scripts: #!/usr/bin/env bash, set -euo pipefail, color output (RED/GREEN/YELLOW vars).

setup-cluster.sh:
- Check docker, k3d, kubectl, helm are installed
- Create k3d cluster: k3d cluster create goldilocks-demo --agents 2
- Install VPA CRDs from kubernetes/autoscaler (latest release tag)
- Install metrics-server via helm: helm install metrics-server metrics-server/metrics-server -n kube-system --set args[0]=--kubelet-insecure-tls
- Wait for metrics-server to be ready
- Print success summary

verify-prerequisites.sh:
- Check k3d >= 5.0, kubectl >= 1.28, helm >= 3.10
- Print PASS/FAIL per tool

verify-goldilocks.sh:
- Check goldilocks-controller pod Running
- Check goldilocks-dashboard pod Running
- Check VPA CRDs exist
- Print PASS/FAIL per check

deploy-sample-app.sh:
- Apply all sample-app/ manifests in order
- Wait for all pods to be Running
- Print pod status summary

cleanup.sh:
- k3d cluster delete goldilocks-demo
- Print confirmation

VALIDATE: Run setup-cluster.sh on the actual machine. Verify clean run from scratch.
tasks.testStrategy: Run setup-cluster.sh on macOS. Verify: k3d cluster goldilocks-demo exists, kubectl get nodes shows Ready, VPA CRDs (verticalpodautoscalers.autoscaling.k8s.io) exist, metrics-server pod Running in kube-system. All scripts must exit 0.

tasks.title: Sample Application Manifests
tasks.description: Create sample-app/ with 3-service metrics-app having intentionally wrong resource settings + load generator
tasks.details:
sample-app/namespace.yaml: ns metrics-app (do NOT add goldilocks label here — added in lesson 05).

frontend/deployment.yaml:
- image: nginx:stable
- replicas: 2
- resources: requests: {cpu: 500m, memory: 512Mi}, limits: {cpu: 500m, memory: 512Mi} (intentionally over-provisioned — Guaranteed QoS)
- Add ConfigMap with custom nginx.conf serving a simple HTML page

frontend/service.yaml: ClusterIP on port 80

api/deployment.yaml:
- image: kennethreitz/httpbin
- replicas: 2
- resources: requests: {cpu: 10m, memory: 16Mi}, limits: {cpu: 100m, memory: 64Mi} (intentionally under-provisioned)

api/service.yaml: ClusterIP on port 80

worker/deployment.yaml:
- image: python:3.11-alpine
- command: [python3, -c, 'import time,math; [math.factorial(10000) for _ in iter(int, 1)]']
- resources: requests: {cpu: 50m, memory: 32Mi}, limits: {cpu: 1000m, memory: 128Mi} (mismatched — Burstable)

load-generator/deployment.yaml:
- image: busybox
- command that runs: while true; do wget -q -O /dev/null http://frontend/; wget -q -O /dev/null http://api/get; sleep 1; done
- resources: requests: {cpu: 10m, memory: 16Mi}

sample-app/README.md: Explain all 3 intentional misconfigurations and why they're educational.

VALIDATE: kubectl apply -f sample-app/ (in order: namespace, then each service). All pods reach Running state.
tasks.testStrategy: kubectl apply -f sample-app/ -R after cluster setup. Verify: kubectl get pods -n metrics-app shows all pods Running. Load generator should be hitting frontend and api endpoints continuously.

tasks.title: Manifests Directory
tasks.description: Create manifests/ with VPA references, Goldilocks Helm values, and before/after recommendation examples
tasks.details:
manifests/00-vpa/README.md: Instructions for installing VPA from kubernetes/autoscaler, with the exact kubectl apply command pointing to the latest release.

manifests/01-goldilocks/values.yaml:
```yaml
image:
  repository: us-docker.pkg.dev/fairwinds-ops/oss/goldilocks
  tag: v4.15.1
vpa:
  enabled: false
metrics-server:
  enabled: false
controller:
  enabled: true
dashboard:
  enabled: true
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    port: 80
```

manifests/02-recommendations/frontend-patched.yaml: frontend deployment with corrected resources (from example VPA recommendation).
manifests/02-recommendations/api-patched.yaml: api deployment with corrected resources.
manifests/README.md: Apply order guide.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify values.yaml is valid YAML. Verify before/after deployments are valid Kubernetes manifests with kubectl --dry-run=client.

tasks.title: Lesson 01 — The Resource Requests Problem
tasks.description: Create 01-intro-resource-problem/index.html, README.md, QUIZ.md. Theory lesson, no cluster needed.
tasks.details:
README.md sections:
- Overview: Introduce the metrics-app team narrative
- Why resource requests matter: scheduler decisions, node capacity
- OOMKills vs CPU throttling: differences, symptoms, detection with kubectl describe pod
- Cost implications: over-provisioning wastes money, under-provisioning causes instability
- QoS classes: BestEffort (no requests/limits), Burstable (partial), Guaranteed (requests=limits)
- Show QoS class in output: kubectl get pod -o jsonpath='{.status.qosClass}'
- The Goldilocks solution preview: what's coming in the series

QUIZ.md: 4 questions:
1. What happens to a pod when it exceeds its memory limit? (OOMKilled)
2. What QoS class is assigned when requests == limits? (Guaranteed)
3. What does CPU throttling cause? (Slow performance, not termination)
4. Why is over-provisioning expensive? (Wastes cluster capacity, inflates node costs)

Duration: ~10 min. Level: Beginner. No prerequisites beyond basic kubectl knowledge.
tasks.testStrategy: No cluster validation needed. Review content for accuracy. Verify QUIZ.md has correct answers. Verify Docsify renders from index.html.

tasks.title: Lesson 02 — Local Kubernetes Setup with k3d
tasks.description: Create 02-local-k8s-setup/index.html, README.md, QUIZ.md. Full cluster setup walkthrough.
tasks.details:
README.md sections:
- Prerequisites: Docker Desktop running, Homebrew (macOS) or package manager
- Install k3d: brew install k3d (macOS) + Linux alternative
- Install kubectl: brew install kubectl
- Install helm: brew install helm
- Create cluster: k3d cluster create goldilocks-demo --agents 2
- Verify cluster: kubectl get nodes (should show 3 nodes: server + 2 agents)
- Install VPA CRDs: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/releases/download/vertical-pod-autoscaler-<VERSION>/vpa-v1-crd-gen.yaml (get latest tag)
- Install VPA admission controller + updater + recommender from autoscaler
- Install metrics-server: helm repo add metrics-server https://kubernetes-sigs.github.io/metrics-server/ && helm install metrics-server metrics-server/metrics-server -n kube-system --set args[0]=--kubelet-insecure-tls
- Verify VPA pods running: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep vpa
- Verify metrics-server: kubectl top nodes
- Reference setup-cluster.sh for automated setup

QUIZ.md: 4 questions on k3d, VPA purpose, why metrics-server is needed, what --kubelet-insecure-tls does

VALIDATE: Every command in README must be tested. kubectl top nodes must show metrics.
Duration: ~20 min. Level: Beginner.
tasks.testStrategy: CRITICAL: Run every command from this lesson in order on a fresh k3d cluster. Verify kubectl top nodes works. Verify VPA pods (recommender, updater, admission-controller) are Running in kube-system.

tasks.title: Lesson 03 — VPA Fundamentals
tasks.description: Create 03-vpa-fundamentals/index.html, README.md, QUIZ.md. Teach VPA before introducing Goldilocks.
tasks.details:
README.md sections:
- What is VPA: Vertical vs Horizontal scaling
- VPA modes: Off (recommend only), Initial (set on pod creation), Recreate (evict+recreate), Auto (same as Recreate)
- Why Goldilocks uses Off mode: recommendations without disruption
- Create a manual VPA for the api deployment (deploy sample-app first):
```yaml
apiVersion: autoscaling.k8s.io/v1
kind: VerticalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: api-vpa-manual
  namespace: metrics-app
spec:
  targetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: api
  updatePolicy:
    updateMode: 'Off'
  resourcePolicy:
    containerPolicies:
    - containerName: '*'
      minAllowed:
        cpu: 10m
        memory: 16Mi
```
- Read VPA recommendation: kubectl describe vpa api-vpa-manual -n metrics-app
- Show the lowerBound/target/upperBound in raw kubectl output
- Explain why early recommendations may be inaccurate (need historical data)

QUIZ.md: 4 questions on VPA modes, why Off mode doesn't evict pods, difference between VPA and HPA

VALIDATE: VPA object created, kubectl describe shows recommendation section (even if minimal after short time)
Duration: ~15 min.
tasks.testStrategy: Apply the manual VPA YAML. Wait 5 minutes. kubectl describe vpa api-vpa-manual -n metrics-app must show the recommendation section with at least some values (even if unreliable at this point).

tasks.title: Lesson 04 — Installing Goldilocks
tasks.description: Create 04-install-goldilocks/index.html, README.md, QUIZ.md. Helm install with new registry.
tasks.details:
README.md sections:
- Architecture overview: Goldilocks controller + dashboard, how they work together
- Add Helm repo: helm repo add fairwinds-stable https://charts.fairwinds.com/stable && helm repo update
- Create namespace: kubectl create namespace goldilocks
- Install with custom values (new registry):
```bash
helm install goldilocks fairwinds-stable/goldilocks \
  --namespace goldilocks \
  --version 10.4.0 \
  --set image.repository=us-docker.pkg.dev/fairwinds-ops/oss/goldilocks \
  --set image.tag=v4.15.1 \
  --set vpa.enabled=false \
  --set metrics-server.enabled=false
```
- Wait for pods: kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app.kubernetes.io/name=goldilocks -n goldilocks --timeout=120s
- Verify: kubectl get pods -n goldilocks
- Explain what goldilocks-controller does: watches namespaces with label, creates VPA objects
- Explain what goldilocks-dashboard does: reads VPA objects, presents recommendations
- IMPORTANT NOTE: Call out the registry change from quay.io to us-docker.pkg.dev

QUIZ.md: 4 questions on Goldilocks architecture, what happens without the namespace label, what the controller creates

VALIDATE: helm install succeeds with new registry, both pods reach Running state.
Duration: ~15 min.
tasks.testStrategy: CRITICAL: Run helm install with exact values.yaml. Verify kubectl get pods -n goldilocks shows goldilocks-controller and goldilocks-dashboard both in Running state. If image pull fails, the registry path is wrong.

tasks.title: Lesson 05 — Enabling Namespaces and Observing VPAs
tasks.description: Create 05-enable-and-observe/index.html, README.md, QUIZ.md. Label namespace, watch VPAs created.
tasks.details:
README.md sections:
- Deploy metrics-app: kubectl apply -f sample-app/ (or reference deploy-sample-app.sh)
- Verify pods running: kubectl get pods -n metrics-app
- Enable namespace for Goldilocks: kubectl label ns metrics-app goldilocks.fairwinds.com/enabled=true
- Watch VPA objects appear: kubectl get vpa -n metrics-app -w (should see frontend, api, worker VPAs created within 30-60s)
- Inspect a VPA: kubectl describe vpa goldilocks-frontend -n metrics-app
- Show the connection: label → controller watches → VPA objects created
- Note: VPA naming convention used by Goldilocks: goldilocks-<deployment-name>
- Check controller logs: kubectl logs -n goldilocks -l app.kubernetes.io/component=controller --tail=20

QUIZ.md: 4 questions on what happens when the label is added, VPA naming convention, what the controller logs show

VALIDATE: After labeling, kubectl get vpa -n metrics-app shows 3 VPAs (goldilocks-frontend, goldilocks-api, goldilocks-worker) within 60s.
Duration: ~15 min.
tasks.testStrategy: Label namespace, time how long VPAs take to appear. Must be < 60s. kubectl get vpa -n metrics-app must show all 3 deployments. kubectl describe vpa goldilocks-api -n metrics-app must show targetRef pointing to api deployment.

tasks.title: Lesson 06 — The Goldilocks Dashboard
tasks.description: Create 06-goldilocks-dashboard/index.html, README.md, QUIZ.md. Port-forward and read recommendations.
tasks.details:
README.md sections:
- Port-forward dashboard: kubectl -n goldilocks port-forward svc/goldilocks-dashboard 8080:80
- Open http://localhost:8080
- Dashboard overview: namespace selector, deployment list, recommendation columns
- Understanding columns: Current Request, Guaranteed (target), Burstable (lowerBound/upperBound)
- Reading frontend recommendations: VPA will suggest much lower CPU/memory (it's over-provisioned)
- Reading api recommendations: VPA will suggest higher CPU/memory (it's under-provisioned)
- Understanding QoS implications: Guaranteed = requests==limits, Burstable = requests<limits
- When to use Guaranteed: Production stateful services, predictable workloads
- When to use Burstable: Variable workloads, cost savings at risk of throttling
- Screenshot description: Walk learner through UI elements they should see
- Important caveat: Wait at least 10-15 min with load generator running before recommendations are meaningful

QUIZ.md: 4 questions on Guaranteed vs Burstable trade-offs, when recommendations become accurate, what lowerBound means

VALIDATE: Dashboard accessible at localhost:8080 with metrics-app namespace showing. All 3 deployments visible with recommendation values.
Duration: ~20 min.
tasks.testStrategy: Port-forward dashboard. Navigate to http://localhost:8080. Verify metrics-app namespace appears in the dropdown. Verify frontend, api, and worker deployments all show recommendation values (not blank). Document actual recommendation values for Lesson 07.

tasks.title: Lesson 07 — Applying Recommendations
tasks.description: Create 07-applying-recommendations/index.html, README.md, QUIZ.md. Apply Guaranteed for frontend, Burstable for api.
tasks.details:
README.md sections:
- Reading dashboard values for frontend (over-provisioned): note the Guaranteed recommendation
- Updating frontend deployment to use Guaranteed values (requests == limits):
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment frontend \
  -n metrics-app \
  --containers=nginx \
  --requests=cpu=<GUARANTEED_CPU>,memory=<GUARANTEED_MEM> \
  --limits=cpu=<GUARANTEED_CPU>,memory=<GUARANTEED_MEM>
```
- Watching rollout: kubectl rollout status deployment/frontend -n metrics-app
- Reading dashboard values for api (under-provisioned): note Burstable recommendation
- Updating api deployment with Burstable values (lowerBound for request, upperBound for limit)
- Verifying QoS class after apply: kubectl get pod -n metrics-app -l app=frontend -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.qosClass}'
- Should show Guaranteed for frontend, Burstable for api
- Using goldilocks summary: kubectl exec -n goldilocks deployment/goldilocks-dashboard -- goldilocks summary
- Show JSON output and how to pipe to jq
- Before/after resource comparison table

QUIZ.md: 5 questions on QoS class verification, what triggers a pod restart, Guaranteed vs Burstable cost trade-off, kubectl set resources vs patching YAML

VALIDATE: After applying, kubectl describe pod -n metrics-app -l app=frontend shows new resource values. QoS class == Guaranteed.
Duration: ~20 min.
tasks.testStrategy: Apply resources to frontend and api deployments. Verify rollout completes. Verify QoS class with kubectl get pod jsonpath. Compare kubectl top pod -n metrics-app before/after to see if over-provisioned frontend now uses much less.

tasks.title: Lesson 08 — Advanced Configuration
tasks.description: Create 08-advanced-configuration/index.html, README.md, QUIZ.md. Per-namespace modes, container exclusions.
tasks.details:
README.md sections:
- Per-namespace VPA update mode (usually used for testing, not production):
  kubectl annotate ns metrics-app goldilocks.fairwinds.com/vpa-update-mode=off
- Container exclusions for sidecars:
  kubectl label deployment api goldilocks.fairwinds.com/exclude-containers=sidecar-name -n metrics-app
- VPA resource policy (min/max bounds) via namespace annotation:
  kubectl annotate ns metrics-app 'goldilocks.fairwinds.com/vpa-resource-policy={"containerPolicies":[{"containerName":"*","minAllowed":{"cpu":"10m","memory":"16Mi"},"maxAllowed":{"cpu":"1000m","memory":"1Gi"}}]}'
- Controller --on-by-default mode (explain via Helm values, not actual change):
  Show values.yaml snippet with controller.flags: {on-by-default: true}
- Excluding namespaces with on-by-default:
  Show controller.flags: {exclude-namespaces: kube-system,kube-public}
- Image signature verification:
  cosign verify us-docker.pkg.dev/fairwinds-ops/oss/goldilocks:v4.15.1 --key https://artifacts.fairwinds.com/cosign-p256.pub

QUIZ.md: 4 questions on when to use exclude-containers, what on-by-default enables, VPA resource policy purpose

VALIDATE: Resource policy annotation applied, verify new VPA object respects min/max bounds in kubectl describe vpa.
Duration: ~15 min. Level: Intermediate.
tasks.testStrategy: Apply VPA resource policy annotation. Delete and re-check VPA object. Verify kubectl describe vpa shows ContainerPolicies with minAllowed/maxAllowed from annotation.

tasks.title: Lesson 09 — Production Considerations
tasks.description: Create 09-production-considerations/index.html, README.md, QUIZ.md. 8-day convergence, CI/CD, multi-namespace.
tasks.details:
README.md sections:
- The 8-day convergence: VPA needs ~8 days of historical data for accurate recommendations. lowerBound converges faster than upperBound. Early recommendations may be unrealistic (e.g., 100T memory).
- Monitoring convergence: Check VPA lastUpdateTime, track changes over days
- When NOT to use Goldilocks:
  * Jobs and CronJobs (ephemeral, no steady-state)
  * Batch workloads with highly variable patterns
  * Pods with extreme traffic spikes (use HPA instead)
- Multi-namespace strategy: Label multiple namespaces, use --on-by-default cautiously
- CI/CD integration: goldilocks summary JSON output
  kubectl exec -n goldilocks deploy/goldilocks-dashboard -- goldilocks summary | jq '.namespaces["metrics-app"]'
- Example: Compare current vs recommended in a pipeline script
- Related Fairwinds tools: Polaris (policy), Nova (chart updates), Pluto (deprecated APIs)
- VPA Auto mode vs Goldilocks Off mode: when to graduate to Auto
- Commercial option: Fairwinds Insights for multi-cluster

QUIZ.md: 4 questions on 8-day convergence, when to exclude batch jobs, CI/CD integration approach

No cluster validation needed for most of this lesson (conceptual). goldilocks summary command should be validated.
Duration: ~15 min. Level: Intermediate.
tasks.testStrategy: Validate goldilocks summary command works: kubectl exec -n goldilocks deploy/goldilocks-dashboard -- goldilocks summary. Output should be valid JSON. Test jq parsing.

tasks.title: Goldilocks Cheatsheet
tasks.description: Create goldilocks-cheatsheet/index.html and README.md with comprehensive command reference
tasks.details:
README.md sections:
1. Quick Install (all commands in one block)
2. Namespace Management (label/unlabel, check labels)
3. VPA Inspection (get, describe, watch)
4. Dashboard Access (port-forward command)
5. CLI Commands (goldilocks summary, version, create-vpas, delete-vpas)
6. Configuration Labels & Annotations (all goldilocks.fairwinds.com/* labels with descriptions)
7. Helm Chart Key Values (table: value, default, description)
8. Troubleshooting Commands:
   - Check controller logs
   - Verify VPA CRDs: kubectl get crd | grep autoscaling.k8s.io
   - Check metrics API: kubectl get apiservice v1beta1.metrics.k8s.io
   - Verify VPA recommender: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep vpa
9. Before/After Deployment Examples (Guaranteed and Burstable)
10. Common Patterns table

This is a reference doc, not a lesson — no quiz.
tasks.testStrategy: All commands in cheatsheet verified to work. kubectl commands validated against live cluster. YAML examples pass kubectl --dry-run=client.

tasks.title: Killercoda Scenarios
tasks.description: Add goldilocks course with 3 scenarios to /Users/alma/dev-projects/killercoda-scenarios/
tasks.details:
1. Update killercoda-scenarios/structure.json: add {"path": "goldilocks"} to items array.

2. Create goldilocks/structure.json:
```json
{"title": "Goldilocks: Right-Sizing Kubernetes Resources", "description": "Learn to use Goldilocks to get resource recommendations just right", "items": [{"path": "scenario-1-install-goldilocks"}, {"path": "scenario-2-enable-and-observe"}, {"path": "scenario-3-apply-recommendations"}]}
```

3. scenario-1-install-goldilocks/:
- index.json: title, description, 4 steps, backend: kubernetes-kubeadm-1node
- background.sh: kubectl create namespace goldilocks; kubectl create namespace metrics-app
- intro.md: Context about resource management problem
- step1/ (text.md: Add Helm repo, verify.sh: helm repo list | grep fairwinds)
- step2/ (text.md: Install VPA CRDs, verify.sh: kubectl get crd verticalpodautoscalers.autoscaling.k8s.io)
- step3/ (text.md: Install Goldilocks, verify.sh: kubectl get pods -n goldilocks | grep Running | wc -l | grep -q 2)
- step4/ (text.md: Verify both pods running, verify.sh: same as step3)
- finish.md: Summary

4. scenario-2-enable-and-observe/:
- background.sh: Full Goldilocks install + deploy metrics-app manifests
- 3 steps: deploy metrics-app, label namespace, inspect VPA objects
- verify.sh for step2: kubectl get vpa -n metrics-app | grep -c goldilocks

5. scenario-3-apply-recommendations/:
- background.sh: Full setup with load running for a few minutes, basic recommendations available
- 4 steps: read VPA recommendation, patch deployment, verify rollout, check QoS class
- verify.sh for step4: kubectl get pods -n metrics-app -l app=frontend -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.qosClass}' | grep -q Guaranteed

All verify.sh scripts: #!/bin/bash, exit 0 on success, exit 1 on failure. No set -e.
tasks.testStrategy: Review all verify.sh scripts for correctness. Manually trace through each scenario step. Verify background.sh installs reliably (important for Killercoda cold start). Test JSON files are valid with python3 -m json.tool.

tasks.title: Quiz Content Upload to Devoriales
tasks.description: Upload all quiz questions from all 9 lessons to devoriales.com/quiz/admin/questions/create using Playwright
tasks.details:
For each lesson (01-09), upload 4-5 questions to https://devoriales.com/quiz/admin/questions/create.
Challenge link: https://devoriales.com/quiz/admin/quiz/21/edit
Use the devoriales-question-creator skill with Playwright MCP.
Each question:
- Practical/scenario-based (not trivia)
- 4 options, 1 correct answer
- Clear explanation for correct answer
- Tagged to the appropriate lesson
Total: ~36-45 questions across 9 lessons.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify each question appears in the Devoriales admin after upload. Check formatting looks correct in the quiz preview.
